title: Ask the Remodeler: How to keep melted snow out of your basement
author: Mark Philben -- Globe Correspondent
bullets: Experts recommend clearing basement window wells of snow and considering removable covers to prevent water entry.
Ensure gutters and downspouts are clear and direct water away from the home's foundation to prevent basement flooding.
Consider installing sump pumps or French drains as long-term basement water management solutions.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a quiz to test knowledge of recent Greater Boston news events.
The quiz is designed to keep readers informed on trending stories in the region.
Peter Chianca, Boston.com's general assignment editor, oversees the content since 2019.

title: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers vote to authorize strike as contract negotiations stall
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Coolidge Corner Theatre workers in Brookline, MA, voted to authorize a strike amid stalled contract negotiations.
The union, including 40 employees, is negotiating its first contract after being recognized in June 2024.
Concerns include job protection and the impact of self-service kiosks; no strike is imminent, says union spokesperson.

title: Alex Cora believes this Red Sox player will slug 30 home runs this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora predicts Wilyer Abreu will hit 30 home runs in the 2026 MLB season.
FanGraphs' 2026 projections show no Red Sox player exceeding 20 home runs, with Abreu predicted for 18.
Abreu hit 22 home runs last season; he showcased power in the 2026 World Baseball Classic for Venezuela.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots taking ‘fascinating’ offensive tackle with pick No. 31
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots are projected to select offensive tackle Max Iheanachor with the 31st pick in the 2026 NFL Draft.
Iheanachor, from Arizona State, is seen as a potential long-term replacement for veteran tackle Morgan Moses.
Standing at 6-foot-6 and 321 pounds, Iheanachor was a starting tackle and earned All-Big 12 honors in 2025.

title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Worcester officials addressed racist comments following a March 6 basketball game between South High and Andover High schools.
NAACP and YWCA denounced social media threats and racial abuse targeting South High players, calling it unacceptable and dehumanizing.
A viral video of the game, with offensive captions, was removed from Facebook; similar content remains on Instagram with comments disabled.

title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christopher Mota, 29, was charged with sexual exploitation of children for exploiting minors aged 12 to 16 years.
Authorities found hundreds of illegal images and videos in a hidden folder on Mota's phone during a border search.
Mota admitted to obtaining child sexual abuse material from minors via text, Snapchat, and Telegram, paying them through cash apps.

title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened Thursday after a water main break caused extensive flooding.
The Boston Water and Sewer Commission clarified a 12-inch pipe rupture caused the flood, initially thought to be a 36-inch main.
Several businesses and three daycares evacuated due to the flood, with traffic and busway temporarily rerouted.

title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Derrick Todd, 52, faces three new rape charges and 17 indecent assault counts involving 11 patients aged 20 to 60.
The alleged assaults occurred from 2021 to 2023 at Charles River Medical Associates in Framingham, officials say.
Todd resigned from Brigham and Women's in 2023 amid an investigation; over 200 patients joined a class action lawsuit.

title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, advises adding a second battery or solar panels for van conversions without space for a gas generator.
Conversion suggestions include using a pure sine wave converter and determining wattage needs for electronics and appliances.
Charging the auxiliary battery can be done with the vehicle alternator or preferably with rooftop solar panels and a voltage regulator.
